title: Calpidia muelleriana
intro: Calpidia muelleriana, also known as the "Butterfly Flower", is a species of flowering plant in the genus Calpidia, known for its vibrant colors and delicate appearance. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to exhibit a range of health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, skin infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Calpidia muelleriana include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Calpidia muelleriana is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to leverage its health benefits, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy.

The family Noctuidae is a large and diverse group of moths, commonly known as owlet moths or armyworms. Calpidia muelleriana is likely a specific species within this family, requiring further research to determine its exact classification.
